instruction: Request Supabase credentials from the human user. Explain that a free Supabase project is required at https://supabase.com. Ask for exactly three values: (1) Project URL (e.g. https://xxx.supabase.co), (2) anon public key (Settings > API > anon public), (3) PostgreSQL connection string in URI format (Settings > Database > Connection string > URI, Transaction pooler, port 6543, replace [YOUR-PASSWORD] with the actual db password).

Request Airbyte Cloud credentials from the human user. Explain that a free Airbyte Cloud account is required at https://cloud.airbyte.com. Ask for exactly two values: (1) API key (Settings > API keys > New API key), (2) workspace ID (visible in the Airbyte Cloud URL: app.airbyte.com/workspaces/<workspace_id>).
Connect to the Supabase PostgreSQL DWH using the credentials received in task 1. Substitute the actual Project URL, anon key, and PostgreSQL connection string from your working memory.

Configure the Airbyte client and create a PostgreSQL destination in Airbyte Cloud pointing to the Supabase database. Use the Airbyte API key and workspace ID from task 2. The destination_config must use destination_definition_id '25c5221d-dce2-4163-ade9-739ef790f503' (PostgreSQL). Extract the Supabase host from the postgres URL (format: db.<ref>.supabase.co), use port 5432 for the direct connection, database 'postgres', username 'postgres', password from the postgres URL, schema 'public', ssl_mode {mode: require}.

Create an Airbyte Faker (Sample Data) source and a connection from that source to the Supabase destination created in task 4. The source uses definition_id 'e1ead99e-0f8e-4f56-a8e7-5f6c2bb0a7e6' (Sample Data / Faker). Configure the source to sync 3 streams: users, products, purchases. The destination_id comes from the task 4 result.

Trigger the Airbyte sync for the connection created in task 5. Wait until the sync completes. The connection_id comes from the task 5 result. This will load the Faker users, products, and purchases tables into the Supabase public schema.

Configure the dbt postgres profile to connect to the Supabase PostgreSQL database. Use db_type='postgres'. Extract host (db.<ref>.supabase.co), user (postgres), password and dbname (postgres) from the Supabase credentials in task 1. Use port 5432 (direct connection) and schema 'public'.

Generate and run a dbt transformation model for DIM_CUSTOMER. Source table 'users' in Supabase (loaded by Airbyte in task 6) has columns: id, name, email, gender, address, created_at, updated_at, age, company, phone, title, height, nationality. Select: id as customer_id, name, email, gender, age, company. Reference the source table as plain name 'users' (it is in the public schema targeted by the dbt profile).

Generate and run a dbt transformation model for DIM_PRODUCT. Source table 'products' in Supabase (loaded by Airbyte in task 6) has columns: id, name, make, year, price, created_at, updated_at. Select: id as product_id, name as product_name, make, year, price. Reference the source table as plain name 'products'.

Generate and run a dbt transformation model for DIM_DATE. Source table 'purchases' in Supabase (loaded by Airbyte in task 6) has a created_at timestamp column. Extract distinct dates from purchases.created_at: date_key (integer YYYYMMDD), full_date, year, month, day, day_of_week. Reference the source table as plain name 'purchases'.

Generate and run a dbt transformation model for FACT_PURCHASES. Source table 'purchases' in Supabase (loaded by Airbyte in task 6) has columns: id, user_id, product_id, quantity, total, store_name, store_id, store_longitude, store_latitude, created_at, updated_at. Select: id as purchase_id, user_id as customer_id, product_id, quantity, total as revenue, store_name, created_at::date as purchase_date. Reference the source table as plain name 'purchases'.
